# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 1078
- **Lines of code**: 349,249
- **Languages**: TypeScript (910), CSS (44), JavaScript (43), Markdown (32), JSON (25)
- **Zones**: 36 (36 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 21786d2
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-09T04:00:32.717Z

## Architecture Zones

### Architecture Docs (`architecture-docs`)

Project-level architectural documentation, decision records, audit reports, and design analysis consumed by developers and AI tooling.

- Files: 20
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/architecture-docs/context.md`

### Crash Recovery Subsystem (`crash-recovery`)

The web viewer's crash detection and graceful recovery subsystem, comprising a crash-detector utility and a React hook that restores viewer state after unhandled errors.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.29, Coupling: 0.71
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/use-crash-recovery.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/performance/crash-detector.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/crash-recovery/context.md`

### E2E Test Suite (`e2e-tests`)

End-to-end tests validating CLI contracts, architecture policy enforcement, and orchestration delegation across all n-dx entry points.

- Files: 22
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/e2e-tests/context.md`

### Hench Agent (`hench-agent`)

Autonomous agent execution engine that picks PRD tasks, builds briefs, drives Claude in a tool-use loop, and records run history.

- Files: 155
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-agent/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/audit
GET     /api/hench/concurrency
GET     /api/hench/config

GET     /api/hench/execute/status/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory
GET     /api/hench/memory/history
GET     /api/hench/memory/history/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory/leaks
GET     /api/hench/metrics
GET     /api/hench/metrics/snapshots
GET     /api/hench/runs
GET     /api/hench/runs/:id
GET     /api/hench/runs/health
GET     /api/hench/task-usage
GET     /api/hench/templates
GET     /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/throttle
POST    /api/hench/execute
POST    /api/hench/execute/:taskId/terminate
POST    /api/hench/runs/:id/mark-stuck
POST    /api/hench/templates
POST    /api/hench/templates/:id/apply
POST    /api/hench/throttle/emergency-stop
POST    /api/hench/throttle/pause
POST    /api/hench/throttle/resume
PUT     /api/hench/config
PUT     /api/hench/throttle
```

POST    /api/hench/workflow/suggestions/:id

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- CLAUDE.md contains an explicit sync notice requiring CODEX.md to mirror several sections, but no automated check enforces this invariant. In a monorepo with multiple active contributors, documentation drift between CLAUDE.md and CODEX.md is likely to go undetected. A CI lint step comparing the two files' mirrored sections would make this a hard failure rather than a manual audit responsibility. [warning] (architecture-docs)
- The runtime bidirectional dependency between crash-recovery and web-dashboard (3 imports each direction) has no interface boundary — crash-recovery directly imports from web-dashboard viewer state and web-dashboard directly imports crash-detector. If crash handling ever needs to support server-side error boundaries (process signals, middleware), the viewer-coupled implementation cannot be reused without extracting a shared interface, and the coupling surface would have to expand into a new zone simultaneously. [warning] (crash-recovery)
- No e2e test file targets the 'ndx init' multi-package initialization chain — cli-contract.test.js and cli-delegation.test.js cover delegation but initialization sequencing (sourcevision init → rex init → hench init) has no dedicated coverage, leaving cross-package init ordering regressions undetected. [warning] (e2e-tests)
- The e2e suite covers orchestration commands exhaustively but hench's autonomous agent loop (ndx work) is tested only through cli-arg-contracts — no e2e test validates end-to-end task selection, brief construction, and LLM dispatch, meaning the highest-value user path has the weakest integration coverage. [warning] (e2e-tests)
- No intra-zone submodule boundary enforcement exists between agent/analysis/, store/, and prd/ subdirectories. The domain-isolation test only enforces cross-package gateway discipline; intra-package layering violations (e.g. agent importing directly from prd/ internals) are undetected. A zone-internal layer test or directory-level import lint rule would catch coupling decay before it affects cohesion scores. [warning] (hench-agent)
- The MCP route layer's two domain gateways are split across two zones: domain-gateway.ts (sourcevision) lives in mcp-route-layer while rex-gateway.ts (rex) lives in web-dashboard. A developer auditing cross-package MCP imports must inspect both zones to see the full surface, weakening the gateway pattern's auditability guarantee. [warning] (mcp-route-layer)
- Execution log rotation policy is undocumented. CLAUDE.md documents that archive.json is capped at 100 batches, but execution-log.jsonl has no stated bound. Without a documented policy, operators have no signal for when to intervene and automated tooling cannot enforce limits. [warning] (rex-runtime-data)
- rex-gateway.ts (the cross-package gateway for rex imports) resides in web-dashboard rather than in the mcp-route-layer zone where domain-gateway.ts (the sourcevision gateway) lives. The one-gateway-per-source-package rule is satisfied in isolation, but the two gateways serving the same consumer (MCP routes) are in different zones, violating the spirit of the auditable import surface the gateway pattern is meant to provide. [warning] (web-dashboard)
- Zone name 'web-landing-scripts' is semantically incorrect for 6 of its 8 files: two root-level analysis scripts (scripts/), two web package integration contract tests, and two viewer unit tests have no relationship to landing-page scripting. The misleading name delays triage for anyone searching for contract-test failures or tooling scripts. [warning] (web-landing-scripts)
- scripts/parse-gateway-exports.mjs and scripts/hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs are repository-wide analysis scripts with no runtime dependency on the web package. Classifying them under a web zone means they will receive web-package zone health thresholds and pin rules that do not apply to them, producing silent mis-governance. [warning] (web-landing-scripts)
- ... and 4 more

### Patterns

- The crash-recovery zone exhibits the 'forced satellite' anti-pattern: two files that don't directly import each other are grouped together because they share a thematic concern (crash handling) rather than an import relationship. The low cohesion (0.29) is a direct consequence. If this zone were dissolved and both files pinned to web-dashboard, the cohesion metric would disappear but architectural clarity would improve. [warning] (crash-recovery)
- e2e-tests zone covers 22 orchestration CLI contracts but domain packages (rex, sourcevision, hench) have no peer e2e zone visible in the zone graph — if domain CLIs are exercised only through unit tests, regressions in their public CLI surface may go undetected until the orchestration layer fails. [warning] (e2e-tests)
- Star topology via rex-gateway.ts is the reference pattern for safe cross-package coupling — one explicit choke point vs the web-viewer hub's many-to-many crossing imports. [info] (hench-agent)
- Anti-corruption layer pattern: mcp-route-layer isolates the MCP protocol surface from rex and sourcevision domain internals via the gateway module. This prevents protocol-level concerns (session IDs, HTTP framing) from leaking into domain logic and vice versa. The pattern is correctly implemented and should be the reference for any future protocol adapter zones. [info] (mcp-route-layer)
- Hub-and-spoke topology: web-dashboard is the composition root that all peer zones import into. Its near-zero outbound coupling (0.02) combined with being the target of every other zone's coupling edges confirms it holds the wiring logic for the entire package. This is intentional but means changes to web-dashboard's public surface ripple across all satellite zones. [info] (web-dashboard)
- This zone is a Louvain residual with no architectural coherence. The three file categories it contains (production, test, tooling) each have distinct owners and should be resolved independently: landing.ts → web-dashboard, integration tests → e2e or web-dashboard test suite, root scripts → project-root zone. Until pins are applied, any zone-level health metrics for this zone are meaningless. [warning] (web-landing-scripts)
- Cross-package residual zone in violation of the documented zone prohibition. PNG logo assets and index.html belong to web-dashboard (viewer static files); tests/integration/ files belong to the project-root or e2e zone. This is the highest-priority zone pin action because cross-package groupings produce unreliable coupling and cohesion metrics that could mask real architectural issues. [warning] (web-static-assets)

### Relationships

- crash-recovery ↔ web-dashboard bidirectional coupling (3 imports each direction) creates a small import cycle. Unlike the mcp-route-layer, which has a type-only reverse edge, crash-recovery's reverse imports are runtime (use-crash-recovery.ts hooks back into viewer state). This makes crash-recovery the only zone in this batch with a true runtime circular dependency with web-dashboard, even if intentional. [warning] (crash-recovery)
- mcp-route-layer → web-dashboard coupling (0.6) flows in one direction only at the zone level: routes-mcp.ts is imported by web-dashboard's start.ts composition root. The reverse edge (web-dashboard → mcp-route-layer) exists only as a type-import (erased at compile time). This asymmetry is healthy — the ACL is consumed by the composition root, not entangled with it. [info] (mcp-route-layer)
- web-dashboard absorbs inbound coupling from three satellite zones (crash-recovery, mcp-route-layer, and residual zones) while keeping its own outbound coupling at 0.02 — an asymmetric hub pattern. If web-dashboard's internal cohesion degrades in future runs, this asymmetry will make it harder to split because all satellites would need re-wiring simultaneously. [info] (web-dashboard)
- All 13 outbound coupling imports target web-dashboard; there are no cross-package imports, confirming the coupling metric is a zone classification artifact rather than an architectural defect. [info] (web-viewer-utilities)
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 79 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- Isolated files — no import edges between 20 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (architecture-docs)
- CLAUDE.md includes an explicit sync notice requiring CODEX.md to mirror several sections; a lint rule or CI check enforcing this sync would prevent drift. [info] (architecture-docs)
- Zero coupling is correct — documentation is consumed by humans and AI context loaders, not imported at runtime. [info] (architecture-docs)
- docs/analysis/ contains several audit documents (process-lifecycle, signal-handling, viewer-audit); these should be revisited after major refactors to avoid stale guidance. [info] (architecture-docs)
- High coupling (0.71) — 3 imports target "web-dashboard" [warning] (crash-recovery)
- Low cohesion (0.29) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (crash-recovery)
- Cohesion 0.29 is below the 0.4 threshold, driven by the small file count and the fact that crash-detector.ts and use-crash-recovery.ts have different primary importers; this is acceptable for a two-file subsystem. [warning] (crash-recovery)
- Coupling 0.71 exceeds the 0.6 warning threshold; this is an artifact of the bidirectional composition-root wiring with web-dashboard documented in zone hints rather than uncontrolled dependency spread. [warning] (crash-recovery)
- Isolating crash detection into its own zone prevents error-handling concerns from leaking into viewer component logic — a clean separation of the unhappy path. [info] (crash-recovery)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (e2e-tests)
- ... and 41 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "architecture-docs" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (architecture-docs)
- packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/ contains files from 3 zones (crash-recovery, web-dashboard, web-viewer-utilities) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Document in zone hints for both web-dashboard and mcp-route-layer that the web package has two gateways in two separate zones. Cross-package gateway audits currently have no machine-readable signal that both zones must be scanned to obtain a complete import surface inventory, making partial audits appear complete. [warning]
- Establish and document a 'web-' naming prefix requirement for all zones whose files reside primarily within the packages/web/ directory. Currently three zones comply and two do not; enforcing this in zone hints or a lint rule would make package ownership discoverable from zone IDs alone and prevent future zones from silently omitting the prefix. [info]
- No automated check prevents new bidirectional import cycles from being added to web-viewer, which already participates in three simultaneous bidirectional cycles (web-dashboard, web-server, crash) — a coupling-cap assertion in domain-isolation.test.js that fails if web-viewer's bidirectional partner count exceeds three would enforce the documented intentional-coupling surface as a hard constraint [warning]
- Standardize zone IDs to full compound nouns: rename 'crash' → 'crash-recovery', 'e2e' → 'e2e-tests', 'logo' → 'logo-assets'. All other multi-word zones already follow this convention; these three abbreviations are the only exceptions and cause ID-name mismatches in documentation references. [info]
- The .js vs .ts test file extension split (e2e-tests zone uses .js, all domain package test zones use .ts) should be resolved by converting e2e tests to TypeScript — this gap means the orchestration tier's integration contracts are type-checked less rigorously than domain-tier unit tests, which inverts the expected risk-to-rigor relationship [warning]
- The architecture-policy.test.js gate lives in the slow e2e suite (spawns processes) but enforces rules (import boundaries, gateway discipline) that can be checked statically — moving policy enforcement to a dedicated fast lint-time zone (e.g. policy-enforcement) would allow CI to fail on architectural violations in seconds rather than minutes [info]
- crash-detector.ts is a framework-agnostic browser platform utility currently classified as [utility] and co-located with a Preact hook in the crash-recovery zone. Consider introducing a 'web-platform-utilities' zone or archetype for framework-free browser API wrappers (sessionStorage, performance APIs) to create an explicit separation between platform-layer code and framework-layer hooks — the same distinction that separates server infrastructure from viewer components in web-dashboard. [info]
- web-viewer participates in three simultaneous bidirectional import cycles with no barrel export controlling its public API surface, while hench-agent achieves zero external coupling through an explicit gateway. Apply barrel-export discipline to web-viewer (src/viewer/index.ts) to align the web package's largest zone with the encapsulation pattern proven effective at the 155-file scale in hench-agent. [warning]
- ... and 41 more

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
